extra.reasoning:
Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b).

rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires the full conjunction C = (ckd_dialysis ∧ renal_impairment ∧ hepatic_impairment ∧ immunosuppressed ∧ seizure_history ∧ diabetes). Otherwise it defaults false. So rec_drug_a = true implies C holds.

rec_drug_b is governed by two rules: guard_suppress_b (priority 90, condition C → rec_drug_b := false) and rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, condition ckd_dialysis → rec_drug_b := true), with default false. Conflict resolution is highest-priority-wins per output.

Whenever C holds (the only situation where rec_drug_a can be true), both b-rules match: guard_suppress_b at priority 90 and rec_b_from_trigger at priority 50 (ckd_dialysis is part of C, so it is true). The highest-priority matching rule wins; 90 > 50, so guard_suppress_b dominates and forces rec_drug_b := false. No tie exists, so order does not matter. Thus under C, rec_drug_b = false, making the conjunction false.

When C does not hold, rec_drug_a = false (default), so the conjunction is false regardless of rec_drug_b's value.

In every input assignment at least one of rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b is false. polypharmacy only affects rec_drug_c (priority 40), which is irrelevant to this property. The property holds over the entire 2^7 input space.
